citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility is not administering vaccines for the goats and deer in accordance with their Attending Veterinarian's Program
of Veterinary Care.
The most recent program of veterinary care available during the inspection prescribes a Clostridium/Tetanus vaccine
"CD/T" every year for the goats and deer. The goats and deer at the facility have not received vaccines in the past year.
The licensee could not recollect the last time the animals received this vaccine. Records could not be located at the
facility.
Failing to follow the veterinarian's preventative care instructions for these animals could lead to preventable diseases, and
is preventing the attending veterinarian from ensuring the animals' good health.
Correct by following your program of veterinary care and ensuring that the attending veterinarian has appropriate authority
to ensure the provision of adequate veterinary care and to oversee the adequacy of other aspects of animal care and use.
Correction date: April 5, 2026.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
The facility did not communicate an adult male turkey's medical issue to their attending veterinarian. The inspector
observed that an adult male turkey had an enlarged crop that was hanging low. There were no feathers present on the
crop. When palpated, the area felt firm and filled with liquid. The animal was taking short and slow steps.
The licensee did not communicate this to the veterinarian because the animal had this appearance for a long time and the
licensee believed it to be normal for the animal.
Failing to communicate timely and accurate information on animals' health, behavior, and well-being, such as this animal's
feather loss and abnormal appearance, is preventing the attending veterinarian from ensuring timely and adequate
veterinary care.
Correct by ensuring a mechanism of direct and frequent communication to the attending veterinarian so that timely and
accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed.
Correction date: March 19, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
2.40(a)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility is not administering vaccines for the goats and deer in accordance with their Attending Veterinarian's Program 
of Veterinary Care.  
 
The most recent program of veterinary care available during the inspection prescribes a Clostridium/Tetanus vaccine 
"CD/T" every year for the goats and deer. The goats and deer at the facility have not received vaccines in the past year. 
The licensee could not recollect the last time the animals received this vaccine. Records could not be located at the 
facility. 
 
Failing to follow the veterinarian's preventative care instructions for these animals could lead to preventable diseases, and 
is preventing the attending veterinarian from ensuring the animals' good health.  
 
Correct by following your program of veterinary care and ensuring that the attending veterinarian has appropriate authority 
to ensure the provision of adequate veterinary care and to oversee the adequacy of other aspects of animal care and use. 
 
Correction date: April 5, 2026. 
2.40(b)(3) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
The facility did not communicate an adult male turkey's medical issue to their attending veterinarian. The inspector 
observed that an adult male turkey had an enlarged crop that was hanging low. There were no feathers present on the 
crop. When palpated, the area felt firm and filled with liquid. The animal was taking short and slow steps. 
 
The licensee did not communicate this to the veterinarian because the animal had this appearance for a long time and the 
licensee believed it to be normal for the animal.  
 
Failing to communicate timely and accurate information on animals' health, behavior, and well-being, such as this animal's 
feather loss and abnormal appearance, is preventing the attending veterinarian from ensuring timely and adequate 
veterinary care.

Correct by ensuring a mechanism of direct and frequent communication to the attending veterinarian so that timely and 
accurate information on problems of animal health, behavior, and well-being is conveyed. 
 
Correction date: March 19, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
